predictions.claim: Streaming-Batch Equivalence: Stream-Propagate (A13) produces identical results to batch PROPAGATE (A1) when using canonical merge order on all tested pipelines.
predictions.measurement: Run both streaming and batch propagation on 5000 perturbation sequences across 50 pipeline topologies. Compare output deltas component-wise.
predictions.falsification: Any single case where streaming output ≠ batch output under canonical merge order for a Fragment F pipeline.

predictions.claim: Adaptive Competitive Ratio: Adaptive-Repair (A14) achieves competitive ratio < c·ln(k) where k is the number of perturbation stages, with c ≤ 3 empirically.
predictions.measurement: Run Adaptive-Repair against offline optimal on 1000 perturbation sequences of varying length (k=10 to 10000). Compute empirical competitive ratio.
predictions.falsification: Competitive ratio exceeds 3·ln(k) for more than 5% of test sequences, or exceeds 5·ln(k) for any sequence.

predictions.claim: Incremental Annihilation Speedup: Incremental-Annihilate (A15) achieves 5-10x speedup over full recomputation of annihilation maps for streams of ≥100 perturbations.
predictions.measurement: Compare wall-clock time of incremental vs. batch annihilation detection on perturbation streams of length 100-10000 across 50 pipeline topologies.
predictions.falsification: Speedup < 3x for streams of length ≥ 100, or incremental version produces incorrect annihilation map.

predictions.claim: Delta Compression Ratio: Compress-Delta (A16) achieves 2-5x compression ratio on typical pipeline perturbations, with higher ratios for schema-heavy changes.
predictions.measurement: Apply Compress-Delta to 10000 perturbations across varied pipeline types. Measure compression ratio as |σ_original|/|σ_compressed| in terms of delta components.
predictions.falsification: Median compression ratio < 1.5x, or compressed delta is not semantically equivalent to original in any test case.

predictions.claim: Cost Concentration: Repair cost under stochastic perturbations concentrates within 1.5x of expected cost with probability ≥ 0.95, matching the sub-exponential tail bound of T19.
predictions.measurement: Sample 10000 perturbations from stochastic model, compute repair cost for each. Verify P(cost > 1.5·E[cost]) < 0.05.
predictions.falsification: More than 5% of samples exceed 1.5x expected cost, or tail behavior is heavier than sub-exponential.

predictions.claim: Bisimulation Verification Overhead: Verifying quantitative bisimulation correctness (T21) adds < 5% overhead to total repair planning time.
predictions.measurement: Measure repair planning time with and without bisimulation verification on 1000 pipeline repair instances. Report overhead as percentage of total planning time.
predictions.falsification: Verification overhead exceeds 10% on more than 20% of test instances, or verification misses a correctness violation.

predictions.claim: Privacy-Utility Tradeoff: Differentially private repair (T22) with ε ≥ 1 produces plans within 1.1x of optimal non-private cost.
predictions.measurement: Run exponential mechanism repair selection with ε ∈ {0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5} on 1000 repair instances. Compare selected plan cost to optimal.
predictions.falsification: For ε ≥ 1, median cost ratio exceeds 1.2x optimal, or privacy guarantee is violated in any run.

predictions.claim: Cross-Perturbation Independence: For ≥ 90% of perturbation pairs, the propagation effects are independent (no interaction amplification beyond additive composition).
predictions.measurement: Generate 5000 pairs of perturbations, propagate each individually and jointly. Measure independence as |cost(σ₁∪σ₂) - cost(σ₁) - cost(σ₂)| / max(cost(σ₁), cost(σ₂)).
predictions.falsification: Independence ratio < 90%, or interaction amplification exceeds 2x additive cost for more than 5% of pairs.

baselines.name: Full Recomputation
baselines.description: Naive baseline that rebuilds entire pipeline from scratch, ignoring perturbation locality. Represents worst-case repair strategy with perfect correctness but maximum cost
baselines.implementation: Execute all stages in topological order using DuckDB. No dependency analysis or selective execution. Serves as correctness oracle for Fragment F validation
baselines.expected_outcome: Perfect correctness (100%), maximum cost (baseline ratio = 1.0). Demonstrates upper bound on repair cost and provides ground truth for correctness verification

baselines.name: Modern dbt with Column-Level Lineage
baselines.description: State-of-practice baseline using dbt 1.6+ with column-level lineage analysis and --select flag for selective recomputation. Represents current production capabilities
baselines.implementation: Deploy dbt-core with column lineage enabled. Use dbt --select +modified_model+ for downstream propagation. Include compilation time and dependency resolution overhead in cost measurement
baselines.expected_outcome: Good correctness for simple perturbations (90-95%), 2-5× higher cost than algebraic approach due to inability to detect annihilation opportunities

baselines.name: DBSP Data-Only IVM
baselines.description: State-of-art incremental view maintenance using DBSP circuits with fixed schema. Represents best-case performance for data-only changes while demonstrating schema evolution limitations
baselines.implementation: Construct DBSP circuits for TPC-DS queries using dbsp-rs. Apply data deltas as Z-set changes. Schema changes cause circuit reconstruction (fallback to full recomputation)
baselines.expected_outcome: Excellent performance for data-only changes (10-100× speedup), complete failure on schema evolution. Demonstrates expressiveness gap our approach addresses

baselines.name: Naive Incremental (Direct Effects Only)
baselines.description: Simplest incremental strategy that recomputes stages with modified inputs but ignores transitive effects. Tests necessity of propagation analysis
baselines.implementation: Identify directly affected stages and recompute them. Do not propagate changes downstream unless input tables are explicitly modified. No delta analysis
baselines.expected_outcome: Fast execution (50% cost) but incorrect results (60-70% correctness). Demonstrates why propagation analysis is essential

baselines.name: Rule-Based Heuristic Repair
baselines.description: Hand-engineered repair logic using pattern matching on perturbation types. Represents pre-algebraic engineering approach with domain-specific optimizations
baselines.implementation: Pattern matching on perturbation types: column add → default value injection + downstream recomputation, type change → casting pipeline + affected stage recomputation, data correction → selective propagation based on hardcoded rules
baselines.expected_outcome: Reasonable performance for common cases (70-80% cost efficiency), failure on compound perturbations (75-85% correctness). Shows algebra necessity for compositionality

baselines.name: No-Algebra Ablation (Critical Control)
baselines.description: Our system architecture without delta algebra - same SQL analyzer, dependency graph, and infrastructure but heuristic planning instead of algebraic reasoning
baselines.implementation: Use identical SQL parsing, lineage analysis, and execution engine. Replace algebraic planner with heuristic rules: recompute downstream for schema changes, use dbt-style selective recomputation for data changes, ignore quality deltas
baselines.expected_outcome: 80-90% of algebraic capability without theory. This is the key experiment: if gap is <20%, algebra's value is primarily theoretical elegance. If gap is >30%, algebra provides measurable engineering benefit

complexity_analysis.propagation: O(|V| + |E| + |V|·|C|·|Δ|) where |V| is pipeline size, |E| is dependencies, |C| is average schema size, |Δ| is delta representation size. Accounts for interaction homomorphism application at each stage.
complexity_analysis.planning_dag: O(|V|·k²·|Δ|) for acyclic topologies using dynamic programming, where k ≤ 20 is maximum fan-out. Corrected from original O(|V|·k²) to include delta size factor as identified by red team.
complexity_analysis.planning_general: O(|V|³) for LP relaxation + O(|V|·k) for randomized rounding. Provides (ln k + 1)-approximation for general topologies with cycles.
complexity_analysis.annihilation: O(|δ_s|·|C| + |δ_d|) per stage for column dependency analysis and data delta inspection. Critical optimization enabling 2-5× cost reduction through stage pruning.
complexity_analysis.fragment_check: O(Σ_{v∈V} |AST(v)|) for syntactic analysis of all stage queries. Linear in total AST size, enables runtime verification of commutation preconditions.
complexity_analysis.total_pipeline: O(|V|·k²·|Δ| + |V|³) for planning + O(Σ_{v∈repair_actions} COST(v)) for execution. Planning overhead typically <1% of execution cost for realistic delta sizes.

modules.name: SQL Semantic Analyzer
modules.description: Parse SQL queries and extract column-level lineage using sqlglot. Handles PostgreSQL dialect with 8 core operators. Conservative sound over-approximation for unrecognized patterns

modules.name: Delta Algebra Engine
modules.description: Core three-sorted algebra implementation with interaction homomorphisms φ and ψ. Handles schema/data/quality delta composition and propagation

modules.name: Repair Planner
modules.description: DP algorithm for acyclic topologies, LP relaxation for general case. Delta annihilation optimization for cost reduction

modules.name: Execution Engine
modules.description: DuckDB-based repair execution with checkpointing and rollback. Atomic table replacement and correctness validation

modules.name: Quality Monitor
modules.description: Batch quality monitoring using KS test and PSI for distribution shift detection. Generates quality deltas for constraint violations

implementation_strategy.testing_strategy: Property-based testing for algebra (Hypothesis framework, 10M+ test cases). Integration testing with TPC-DS corpus. Correctness validation against full recomputation baselines. Performance benchmarking on synthetic topologies.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on 5 critical operators with formal proofs. Property-based testing for remaining operators. Conservative fallbacks for edge cases

risk_assessment.risk: Fragment F Coverage Lower Than Expected
risk_assessment.mitigation: Already reduced target to 60-75%. Honest reporting of exclusions. Bounded commutation still valuable outside Fragment F
Demote to design motivation if proof lacks depth. Three-sorted algebra stands on own merits for compound perturbations
Conservative cost models. Focus on annihilation benefits. Fair comparison with modern dbt capabilities
Two-sorted fallback strategy. Incremental delivery. Core algebra before full system integration
Sound over-approximation strategy. Target ≥95% recall with explicit fallback handling

related_work.ivm: Incremental View Maintenance (IVM) literature provides delta propagation foundations but focuses on single-sorted data deltas. DBSP offers sophisticated Z-set algebra but cannot handle schema evolution due to type parametricity. Our three-sorted approach extends IVM to handle schema changes through interaction homomorphisms - a capability no existing IVM system provides.
related_work.schema_evolution: Schema evolution research (Curino et al., Claypool et al.) focuses on migration correctness but treats data as passive payload. They provide schema mapping operators but no integration with incremental computation. We extend this by making schema deltas first-class algebraic objects that interact with data/quality through homomorphisms.
related_work.data_quality: Data quality monitoring (Great Expectations, Deequ) treats quality as separate concern from pipeline maintenance. Quality constraints are typically checked post-hoc without integration into repair planning. Our quality delta lattice enables compound reasoning about schema changes, data corrections, and quality constraint evolution in unified algebraic framework.
related_work.what_we_extend: We extend IVM with multi-sorted algebra, schema evolution with incremental computation semantics, and data quality with repair-aware constraint handling. The interaction homomorphisms φ and ψ are the novel algebraic mechanisms enabling this unification. No prior work provides formal composition rules for simultaneous schema/data/quality changes.

honest_assessment.novelty: 6-7/10. The three-sorted delta algebra with interaction homomorphisms is a novel application of known algebraic patterns to a new domain. Core mathematical techniques (homomorphisms, coherence conditions) are standard in category theory and universal algebra, but their instantiation for relational repair with complete formal definitions and engineering validation is new. The DBSP impossibility result adds theoretical depth if non-trivial, otherwise contributes little novelty.
honest_assessment.difficulty: 8-9/10. Multiple challenging components: (1) Complete formal definitions for 48 coherence conditions across operator/sort combinations, (2) Sound SQL analysis with ≥95% precision on real queries, (3) Efficient algorithms for NP-hard repair planning, (4) Property-based testing framework for algebraic verification, (5) Integration of theory with working system. Each component is individually difficult; their combination is very challenging.
honest_assessment.feasibility: 7/10 with mitigation strategies. Core algebra and basic repair are feasible. Risk factors: SQL analysis complexity (mitigated by conservative over-approximation), coherence verification scale (mitigated by focusing on 5 critical operators), real-world evaluation challenges (mitigated by synthetic benchmarks). Two-sorted fallback provides safety net if three-sorted proves too complex.
honest_assessment.best_paper_probability: 25-35% at VLDB. Strengths: addresses important practical problem, novel algebraic formulation, working system with evaluation. Risks: theoretical contribution may be seen as incremental application rather than fundamental advance, evaluation limited to single SQL dialect, baseline comparisons need careful fairness. Success depends on demonstrating clear engineering value of algebraic approach through ablation studies.

compound_perturbation_walkthrough.scenario_description: Complete worked example showing three-sorted algebra handling compound perturbation: schema evolution (add tax column), data correction (update order status), and quality constraint addition (tax validation rule)

initial_pipeline_state.orders_table: CREATE TABLE orders (id INT PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10,2), status VARCHAR(20))
downstream_queries: SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id

interaction_homomorphism_application.psi_schema_quality: ψ(ADD_COLUMN(tax, DECIMAL(8,2), 4, DEFAULT amount * 0.08))(CONSTRAINT_ADDED(tax_validation, tax >= 0 AND tax <= amount * 0.25)) = CONSTRAINT_ADDED(tax_validation, tax >= 0 AND tax <= amount * 0.25)
interaction_homomorphism_application.composed_perturbation: (ADD_COLUMN(tax, DECIMAL(8,2), 4, DEFAULT amount * 0.08), UPDATE((2, 102, 75.50, 'completed', 6.04), (2, 102, 75.50, 'shipped', 6.04)), CONSTRAINT_ADDED(tax_validation, tax >= 0 AND tax <= amount * 0.25))

aggregation_query.query: SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id
aggregation_query.schema_propagation: ADD_COLUMN(tax) affects base table but not GROUP BY output (customer_id, SUM(amount) unchanged)
aggregation_query.data_propagation: UPDATE changes row but amount column unchanged (75.50 → 75.50), so SUM(amount) for customer_id=102 remains same
aggregation_query.quality_propagation: Tax constraint doesn't affect aggregation output
aggregation_query.annihilation_result: Delta annihilates: (ε_S, 0_D, ⊥_Q) because no aggregated values change

filter_query.schema_propagation: ADD_COLUMN(tax) → ADD_COLUMN(tax, DECIMAL(8,2), 5, DEFAULT amount * 0.08) (position adjusted for SELECT *)
filter_query.data_propagation: UPDATE changes status from 'completed' to 'shipped', neither matches 'pending' filter
filter_query.result: (ADD_COLUMN(tax, DECIMAL(8,2), 5, DEFAULT amount * 0.08), 0_D, CONSTRAINT_ADDED(tax_validation, tax >= 0 AND tax <= amount * 0.25))

repair_plan_generation.dp_algorithm_trace: Stage 1 (aggregation): annihilates → cost = 0, prune from repair. Stage 2 (filter): non-zero delta → cost = recompute_cost(stage_2), include in repair
repair_plan_generation.cost_comparison: Algebraic repair: 1 stage recomputed. dbt --select: 2 stages recomputed (cannot detect annihilation). Cost ratio: 0.5 (50% savings)
repair_plan_generation.correctness_guarantee: Both queries in Fragment F (deterministic), perfect commutation theorem applies: repair result = full recomputation result

analysis_steps: Schema delta doesn't affect output columns
Data delta interaction: φ(ADD_COLUMN(created_at, ...))(δ_d) adds timestamp to all tuples
Projection removes timestamp: π_{customer_id,amount}(extend_tuples(R, created_at, 5, NOW())) = π_{customer_id,amount}(R)
select_column_independence.annihilation_result: true - stage can be pruned from repair plan
select_column_independence.performance_impact: Downstream propagation stops, potentially saving multiple stages

join_key_preservation.query: SELECT o.id, c.name FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
analysis_steps: Check if notes affects join keys: false
Schema delta doesn't affect join semantics or output projection
Result: δ_d propagates unchanged through join, but notes column removed by projection

analysis_steps: Analyze delta effect: DELETE removes all 'processing' orders
Post-delta predicate satisfiability: no tuples remain with status = 'processing'
Filter result: empty set regardless of additional changes

analysis_steps: Net effect: COUNT increases by 1 for customer_id = 103

three_valued_logic.truth_table: NULL AND TRUE = NULL, NULL AND FALSE = FALSE, NULL OR TRUE = TRUE, NULL OR FALSE = NULL, NOT NULL = NULL
three_valued_logic.comparison_semantics: NULL = NULL is NULL (not TRUE), NULL <> anything is NULL, NULL in arithmetic yields NULL
three_valued_logic.aggregate_behavior: COUNT(*) counts NULLs, other aggregates ignore NULL inputs, COUNT(column) excludes NULLs

null_propagation_through_operators.select_projection: NULL values pass through SELECT unchanged unless transformed by expressions
null_propagation_through_operators.join_semantics: NULL join keys never match (including NULL = NULL), affects INNER vs OUTER join results

null_propagation_through_operators.groupby_treatment: GROUP BY treats NULL as distinct value, creates separate group for NULL keys

schema_evolution_null_interaction.type_coercion_nulls: NULL values preserve NULL through type changes (NULL::INT = NULL::VARCHAR = NULL)

additional_lemmas.name: Error Bound Composability
additional_lemmas.statement: ε_bound = Σ ε_v · Π A_w for non-F stages with per-stage deviation ε_v and downstream amplification A_w.

additional_lemmas.name: Push Composition Distributes Over Interactions
additional_lemmas.statement: push_{g∘f}^D(φ(δ_s)(δ_d)) = push_g^D(φ(push_f^S(δ_s))(push_f^D(φ(δ_s)(δ_d)))). Push through composed operators decomposes into sequential pushes with intermediate interaction application.

additional_lemmas.name: Natural Transformation Composition Preserves Coherence
additional_lemmas.statement: If push_f and push_g satisfy hexagonal coherence (T1), then push_{g∘f} satisfies hexagonal coherence.

additional_lemmas.name: DTRS Measure is Well-Founded
additional_lemmas.statement: The measure μ(δ) = (|ops(δ)|, Σ|args(op)|) is well-founded under lexicographic ordering, and every DTRS rule strictly decreases μ.

additional_lemmas.name: All DTRS Critical Pairs are Joinable
additional_lemmas.statement: The 23 critical pairs arising from overlapping DTRS rule left-hand sides all have a common reduct, establishing local confluence.

additional_lemmas.name: Product Lattice Completeness
additional_lemmas.statement: L = Π_{v∈V} (Δ_S_v × Δ_D_v × Δ_Q_v) is a complete lattice when each component lattice is complete.

additional_lemmas.name: Propagation Operator Preserves Monotonicity
additional_lemmas.statement: If all push operators push_{f_v} are monotone and interaction homomorphisms φ, ψ are monotone, then Φ is monotone on L.

additional_lemmas.name: Abstraction α is Monotone and Extensive
additional_lemmas.statement: For all δ_c ∈ D_c: δ_c ⊑_c γ(α(δ_c)). The abstraction α is monotone: δ₁ ⊑_c δ₂ implies α(δ₁) ⊑_a α(δ₂).

additional_lemmas.name: Concretization γ Preserves Plan Validity
additional_lemmas.statement: If plan_a is valid in the abstract domain D_a, then γ(plan_a) is valid in the concrete domain D_c.

additional_lemmas.name: Normal Form Unique Factorization
additional_lemmas.statement: Every DTRS normal form admits a unique factorization into irreducible (prime) sub-terms.

additional_lemmas.name: Commuting Primes Reorder Freely
additional_lemmas.statement: If prime factors σ_i, σ_j commute (σ_i ∘ σ_j = σ_j ∘ σ_i), they can be reordered in the decomposition without changing semantics.
additional_lemmas.used_by: T11 proof (uniqueness up to commuting factors), T13 proof

additional_lemmas.name: Widening Over-Approximation
additional_lemmas.statement: For all x, y ∈ L: x ⊔ y ⊑ x ∇ y. The widening operator always over-approximates the lattice join.

additional_lemmas.name: Fragment F Bounded Widening Gap
additional_lemmas.statement: For operators in Fragment F, the per-step widening gap satisfies ε_w ≤ 0.02·cost(Δ_e*)/diam(G).

additional_lemmas.name: Push Distributes Over Commuting Composition
additional_lemmas.statement: For commuting deltas σ_a, σ_b: PROPAGATE(G, σ_a ∘ σ_b) = PROPAGATE(G, σ_a) ⊕ PROPAGATE(G, σ_b).

additional_lemmas.name: Abstract DP Reduced Branching
additional_lemmas.statement: In the abstract domain, the DP recurrence has branching factor k (vs k² concrete) because interval-valued costs collapse equivalent choices.

additional_lemmas.name: DTRS Rules are Non-Increasing in Size
additional_lemmas.statement: Every DTRS rewrite rule r: lhs → rhs satisfies |rhs| ≤ |lhs|, with strict inequality for cancellation and simplification rules.

additional_lemmas.name: Static Amplification Factor Computation
additional_lemmas.statement: For each SQL operator f in the supported set, amplification_factor(f) is computable from the operator type and schema alone in O(1) time.

additional_lemmas.name: Spectral Radius Bounds Contraction
additional_lemmas.statement: κ = ρ(A) where A is the amplification matrix. If ρ(A) < 1, the propagation operator Φ is a contraction.

additional_lemmas.name: Sound Component Composition
additional_lemmas.statement: If components C₁,...,C_n are individually sound (output ⊒ specification), then their composition C₁ ∘ ··· ∘ C_n is sound.

additional_lemmas.name: Multiplicative Precision Composition
additional_lemmas.statement: If component Cᵢ has precision factor pᵢ (cost ≤ pᵢ·OPT_i), then the composed system has precision ≤ Π pᵢ.

additional_lemmas.name: Sub-Multiplicativity of Propagation
additional_lemmas.statement: For propagation matrices P₁, P₂ at consecutive pipeline stages, ‖P₁·P₂‖ ≤ ‖P₁‖·‖P₂‖ in the spectral norm, enabling stage-by-stage amplification bounding.
additional_lemmas.used_by: T18 proof (spectral sensitivity bound, inductive step)

additional_lemmas.name: Spectral Propagation Amplification
additional_lemmas.statement: For k-stage propagation, the amplification factor satisfies ρ_k(P) ≤ ρ(P)^k with equality when the propagation matrix is normal. For non-normal matrices, the transient amplification is bounded by κ(P)·ρ(P)^k where κ is the condition number.
additional_lemmas.used_by: T18 proof (spectral sensitivity bound, amplification characterization)

additional_lemmas.name: Bounded Differences for Repair Cost
additional_lemmas.statement: Changing a single perturbation component σᵢ to σ'ᵢ changes the repair cost by at most cᵢ = max_v ω(λ(v))·|R_in(v)|·‖∂push_v/∂σᵢ‖, satisfying the bounded differences condition for concentration inequalities.
additional_lemmas.used_by: T19 proof (expected cost concentration, tail bound derivation)

additional_lemmas.name: Canonical Merge Commutativity
additional_lemmas.statement: Under canonical merge order (D41), MERGE(MERGE(δ₁, δ₂), δ₃) = MERGE(MERGE(δ₁, δ₃), δ₂) for all deltas in Fragment F, ensuring streaming order independence.

additional_lemmas.name: Exponential Threshold Regret Bound
additional_lemmas.statement: The exponential threshold schedule τ_t = τ₀·2^{⌊log₂(t)⌋} achieves cumulative regret O(OPT·log k) against the offline optimal, where k is the sequence length.

additional_lemmas.name: Incremental Annihilation Preservation
additional_lemmas.statement: If δ₁ annihilates at node v and δ₂ does not affect v's annihilation predicates, then δ₁ still annihilates after composing with δ₂. Annihilation is monotone under non-interfering composition.

additional_lemmas.name: Cross-Sort Cancellation Completeness
additional_lemmas.statement: The pairwise cross-sort cancellation check in Compress-Delta (A16) detects all cancellations that can be detected from local information at each node pair.
additional_lemmas.used_by: A16 correctness proof, T22 sensitivity analysis

additional_lemmas.name: Bisimulation Compositionality
additional_lemmas.statement: If (π₁, π₂) ∈ B_ε₁ and (π₂, π₃) ∈ B_ε₂, then (π₁, π₃) ∈ B_{ε₁+ε₂}. The bisimulation relation is transitive up to additive tolerance.

notation_conventions.ε_bound: computable error bound for non-F pipelines

categorical_foundations.summary: The extended ARC framework is organized as a category where objects are pipeline states (schema × data × quality triples) and morphisms are delta transformations. SQL operators define functors between delta categories, and interaction homomorphisms φ, ψ are natural transformations ensuring coherence. The key insight is that T7 (Functorial Coherence) elevates T1 (Hexagonal Coherence) from individual operators to arbitrary compositions, enabling modular reasoning about multi-stage pipelines.

key_concepts.name: Delta Category
key_concepts.description: Category Δ with objects (S, D, Q) ∈ Schema × Data × Quality and morphisms δ = (δ_s, δ_d, δ_q). Composition is the three-sorted compound perturbation operation from T6.

key_concepts.name: Operator Functors
key_concepts.description: Each SQL operator f defines a functor F_f: Δ → Δ mapping delta triples through the push operators. T7 asserts these are actual functors (preserving composition and identity).

key_concepts.name: Interaction Natural Transformations
key_concepts.description: φ and ψ are natural transformations between the identity functor and the operator functors, ensuring that cross-sort effects commute with operator application.

key_concepts.name: Galois Connection
key_concepts.description: The abstraction-concretization pair (α, γ) forms an adjunction between the concrete and abstract delta categories. T10 establishes this and T14 exploits it for complexity reduction.

categorical_foundations.relationship_to_theorems: T7 (functorial coherence) is the categorical generalization of T1. T8 (normal forms) provides canonical morphism representatives. T10 (Galois connection) defines an adjunction. T11 (prime decomposition) gives a unique factorization system. T13 (parallel correctness) exploits the monoidal structure.

convergence_theory.summary: Fixed-point convergence theory for cyclic pipeline topologies, establishing termination guarantees, iteration bounds, and acceleration via widening. The central result (T9) bounds convergence at diam(G)·h iterations for finite lattices and diam(G)·⌈log(1/ε)/log(1/κ)⌉ for metric spaces with contraction factor κ.

key_results.practical_implication: For typical pipelines with diameter 5-20 and lattice height 10-100, expect 50-2000 iterations worst case
Widening reduces 50-2000 iterations to 10-25 with ≤1.2× cost increase

key_results.bound: O(|V|²·|E|) time to compute contraction factor κ
key_results.practical_implication: Enables a priori decision between exact iteration and widening

convergence_theory.expected_iteration_model: E[k*] = c₁·diam(G) + c₂·log(|Δ|) + c₃ where c₁ ≈ 1.5-2.0, c₂ ≈ 0.5-1.0, c₃ ≈ 2-5 (empirically calibrated)

statistical_validation.primary_test: One-sided binomial test for convergence proportion (H₀: p ≤ 0.90, α=0.05, n=500)
statistical_validation.regression_test: OLS fit of k* on diam(G) and log(|Δ|) with R² and bootstrap BCa 95% CI
statistical_validation.comparison_test: Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank for widening vs exact, with rank-biserial correlation
statistical_validation.multiple_comparisons: Holm-Bonferroni correction across convergence-related hypotheses (P20, P24)

spectral_theory.summary: Spectral analysis of delta propagation through pipeline stages, establishing quantitative bounds on error amplification via the propagation matrix formalism.
spectral_theory.main_result: T18: Spectral Sensitivity Bound - amplification bounded by ρ_k(P)·‖σ‖
spectral_theory.relationship_to_core: Provides quantitative refinement of T2 (Bounded Commutation) by characterizing the exact amplification factor through spectral analysis of the propagation operator.

probabilistic_repair.summary: Extension of ARC to handle uncertain and stochastic perturbations, with concentration bounds on repair cost and minimax optimal planning under uncertainty sets.
supporting_lemmas: L42: Bounded Differences for Repair Cost
probabilistic_repair.relationship_to_core: Extends T4 (Cost-Optimal Repair Planning) to the stochastic setting, providing robustness guarantees when perturbations are not exactly known.

compositional_verification.summary: Formal verification framework for repair plans based on quantitative bisimulation, enabling modular correctness proofs for composed pipeline repairs.
compositional_verification.main_result: T21: Quantitative Bisimulation Correctness - exact in F, computable degradation outside F, compositional
compositional_verification.relationship_to_core: Provides a verification methodology complementing the correctness theorems (T1, T2, T17), enabling modular reasoning about repair plan equivalence.

differential_privacy.summary: Integration of differential privacy guarantees into the repair planning process via the exponential mechanism, balancing privacy and repair optimality.
differential_privacy.main_result: T22: Differentially Private Repair - ε-DP with utility guarantee O(ln|Plans|/ε)
differential_privacy.relationship_to_core: Adds privacy dimension to T4 (Cost-Optimal Repair Planning), relevant when pipeline data is sensitive and repair operations must not leak information about specific records.

streaming_algorithms.summary: Streaming and online algorithms for delta propagation and repair planning, enabling real-time processing of perturbation streams.
streaming_algorithms.relationship_to_core: Extends A1 (PROPAGATE) and A3 (DP-REPAIR) to streaming and online settings with provable competitive ratios.

incremental_algorithms.summary: Incremental maintenance algorithms for annihilation detection and delta compression, avoiding full recomputation as new perturbations arrive.
incremental_algorithms.relationship_to_core: Extends A2 (ANNIHILATE) to incremental setting and adds schema-aware compression, improving practical efficiency for long-running pipeline monitoring.
